one_line_summary: Solid architecture content and good coverage, but API reference is mechanically templated and all three docs contain duplicated sections that destroy writing quality.
strengths: Architecture doc has clear mermaid diagram, extension points, and security/concurrency sections
High coverage of kernel/ports symbols via AST extraction
Protocol adherence: all writes inside docs-new/, no commits
weaknesses: API.md uses boilerplate 'Purpose: X. Parameters: see signature' for every symbol — no real descriptions
Signatures rendered without spaces (e.g. 'defsize(self)->int') — ugly and signature_match harness fails 0/2
ARCHITECTURE.md and TUTORIAL.md contain entire duplicated sections (sections 5/6/7 repeat earlier content verbatim) — clear padding artifact
Stray helper scripts (gen_*.py, parse_kernel.py, API_RAW.md) left in worktree root outside docs-new/ (untracked but violates spirit)
Tutorial 'expected output' for stub/hello-world is invented, not verified against actual CLI behavior
